summary: Iteration 3 — Full backend + desktop + mobile regression of the interactive rebuild (preloader, hero with sound toggle, configurator price logic, releases, artists carousel + modal, gallery lightbox, reviews, contact). Backend pytest 4/4 PASS (health, contact create+list with id/no _id, minimal payload defaults, invalid payload 422). Desktop (1920x1080) and Mobile (390x844) Playwright runs ALL PASS. Configurator price logic verified end-to-end: early=1.450, early+addon=1.850, weekend=2.400 (CountUp animates). Hero sound toggle flips label 'Lyd fra' ↔ 'Lyd til'. Artists modal opens with YouTube iframe and closes. Gallery lightbox opens/closes. Contact POST returns 200 and contact-success becomes visible on both viewports. Header smooth-scroll via Lenis works (nav-studie -> #studie top=70 = sticky-header offset). Mobile #artister overflow from iteration 2 is FIXED — no horizontal overflow on any section (docW=390 == winW=390, all 6 sections measured 0→390).

issues: mobile-menu-toggle hit area is 34x34px — below Apple HIG / Material 44x44px recommendation. Functional but tight for thumbs. Carried over from iteration 2, LOW priority.
Third-party Spotify/YouTube iframes log noisy console errors in headless Chromium — explicitly out-of-scope per the request.

action_items: (Optional / LOW) Increase mobile-menu-toggle hit area to >=44x44 (e.g., add min-h-[44px] min-w-[44px] or use p-3 instead of p-2).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is clean: async motor, ObjectId stripped via response_model_by_alias=False, env-driven MONGO_URL/DB_NAME, /api prefix consistent. Pydantic validation enforces required name/email/message (422 on missing). Good.

Configurator.jsx price logic is correct and uses CountUp; testids session-option-<id>, session-addon, configurator-book-btn all present and wired to Planway. Note the price <div> only has class 'text-brand' alongside text-7xl/text-8xl — testing agents should target the price by combined classes, not just '.text-brand' (Section index '02' also uses text-brand).
Preloader.jsx uses AnimatePresence with exit y:-100% and finally setDone(true) — DOM is fully detached (state=detached) within ~2s as documented. Good.

Hero.jsx splits the headline 'Fra idé til hit.' across nested motion spans. body innerText contains both 'Fra idé' and 'til hit.' — text assertions need partial matching. Consider a single data-testid='hero-headline' wrapper for cleaner targeting.
CORS_ORIGINS='*' with allow_credentials=True in backend/.env — harmless here (no auth) but should be tightened before production (browsers reject credentialed wildcard CORS).
Artists.jsx now constrains children correctly — no mobile overflow (previously 412px on a 390px viewport).
seed_data_creation: TEST_-prefixed contacts inserted via pytest (TEST_Patrick QA, TEST_minimal) and Playwright (TEST_Playwright_iter3 desktop, TEST_mobile_iter3 mobile). All persisted in MongoDB contacts collection.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All review items pass. Backend tests at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (4/4). Configurator price selector trick: the big animated price <div> has BOTH 'text-brand' AND 'text-7xl'/'text-8xl' classes — match on the combination, NOT plain '.text-brand' which also matches SectionHeader '02' index. Use window.lenis.scrollTo('#id',{immediate:true}) to bypass smooth-scroll in headless. Custom Cursor is pointer-events:none — safe to ignore. Mobile menu uses unique data-testid='mobile-nav-<id>' (separate from desktop nav-<id>); desktop uses data-testid='nav-<id>'. Mobile #artister overflow fixed in iteration 3.
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found this iteration. Previous iteration's #artister mobile overflow (412px on 390px viewport) has been resolved by the main agent in the rebuild; all 6 sections now report width 0→390 on a 390px viewport.
